title: Use of unsafe operation in safe context
explanation: This error occurs when you perform an unsafe operation (like dereferencing a raw pointer, calling an unsafe function, or accessing a mutable static) without wrapping it in an unsafe block. Rust requires you to explicitly opt into unsafe operations.
error_message: error[E0133]: dereference of raw pointer is unsafe and requires unsafe function or block

fixes.description: Wrap the operation in an unsafe block
fixes.code:
let ptr: *const i32 = &42;
let val = unsafe { *ptr }; // Explicitly opt into unsafe

tip: Keep unsafe blocks as small as possible. Wrap them in safe functions that validate invariants. Document why the unsafe operation is actually safe with a // SAFETY: comment.
